stages.name: release_dogfood_gate
stages.evidence: the final release-evidence manifest validates: the fixture_dogfood, internal_module_dogfood, public_docs_doctest, mutation_aware_doctest, doctest_survivor_ai, pipeline_artifact_validation, and failure_recovery_validation sub-gates all passed with archived or test-verified evidence; the archived deterministic dogfood reports run1.report.json / run2.report.json exist and normalize to identical bytes; the protected scope has no invalid mutants; and the one protected-scope survivor (m_0dogfoodsourcemapaddsub002) is recorded with equivalent-risk review evidence (artifacts/pipeline/085/dogfood/survivor_review.md). The gate self-tests against five invalid manifests (missing dogfood evidence, invalid mutant, nondeterministic comparison, unresolved survivor, missing gate).

stages.name: ci_entrypoint_contract
stages.evidence: scripts/ci.sh now ends with the release_dogfood_gate stage after format_check, build, unit_tests, task_system_validation, pipeline_artifact_validation, and advisory_dogfood; the full scripts/ci.sh runs to 'ci: all required deterministic stages passed' (exit 0), so the final release gate runs before task 060 release acceptance.

stages.name: unit_tests
stages.evidence: test/release_dogfood_gate_test proves the valid manifest passes with archived deterministic dogfood evidence, the gate is rejected without complete deterministic evidence (five invalid manifests), the archive-existence check distinguishes present from absent reports, and scripts/ci.sh + scripts/release_dogfood_gate.py exercise every required sub-gate; full suite green.

stages.name: mutation_checks
stages.reason: task 085 is the release dogfood gate itself: it adds a release-gate script, a Zig gate test, release-evidence fixtures, archived dogfood reports, and CI/dogfood/acceptance docs. It changes no mutator or deterministic-core decision code, so it is not a self-mutation target; the dogfood it gates already covers the protected production scope.

residual_risk: The release-evidence manifest is the executable record of the final gate; the dogfood sub-gates that produce archived reports (fixture/internal-module) are validated by file existence plus the deterministic run1/run2 normalization, while the doctest/survivor/artifact/recovery sub-gates are recorded as test-verified (verified_by) because they are enforced by the Zig suite and the task-system validator rather than a standalone archived report.

The one protected-scope survivor is recorded with deterministic equivalent-risk review evidence rather than a new pinning test; a follow-up unit test of the exact source-map offset arithmetic is noted for task 060 release acceptance. AI played no role in the survivor classification.

scripts/release_dogfood_gate.py and the Zig gate test reuse the deterministic dogfood reports archived from task 059's selected production scope; broadening the dogfood scope or adding new mutators is out of scope for this gate (non-goals).
